content.content:
1	---
2	name: init-workspace
3	description: Initialize a new workspace OR verify and repair an existing one. Detects mode from the current directory — runs guided setup if no workspace is found, runs health checks and fixes if one already exists.
4	---
5	
6	## Task
7	
8	Two modes — detect which applies before doing anything else.
9	
10	---
11	
12	## Mode detection
13	
14	Check whether the current directory is an existing workspace:
15	
16	1. Does `./workspace.yaml` exist?
17	2. Does `./CLAUDE.md` exist?
18	
19	| Condition | Mode |
20	|---|---|
21	| Both exist | **Verify & repair** — check and fix the existing workspace |
22	| Neither or only one exists | **Create** — run the guided setup wizard |
23	
24	Do not ask the user which mode to use. Detect it from the filesystem.
25	
26	---
27	
28	## Mode A — Verify & repair (existing workspace)
29	
30	When the current directory is an existing workspace, verify its health and fix anything missing or broken. Work through each check in order. Report a summary at the end.
31	
32	### Check 1 — `.env` file exists
33	
34	- If `.env` is missing and `.env.template` exists: offer to create `.env` from the template. Do so if the user agrees.
35	- If both are missing: create `.env` from scratch using the template structure.
36	
37	### Check 2 — Required variables populated
38	
39	Read `.env` and check each required variable from the table in **Required vs. Deferrable**. Also read `workspace.yaml` to discover which `<REPO_ID_UPPER>_LOCAL_PATH` variables are expected (one per repo in the `repos:` list).
40	
41	For each missing or empty required variable:
42	- Tell the user which variable is missing and which skill requires it.
43	- Run the relevant phase from the guided wizard (Phase 3 for git identity, Phase 4 for GitHub/SSH, etc.) to collect the value.
44	- Write the value into `.env` immediately — do not batch; fix as you go.
45	
46	Do not prompt for deferrable variables unless the user asks.
47	
48	### Check 3 — `.env.template` in sync
49	
50	Compare `.env.template` against `.env`. For any variable key present in `.env.template` but missing or empty in `.env`, warn the operator and prompt them to add it (following the same flow as Check 2).
51	
52	Do **not** modify `.env.template` automatically. The template is the source of truth — the sync direction is `.env.template` → `.env`, never the reverse. Keys that exist in `.env` but are absent from `.env.template` are not a problem and require no action.
53	
54	### Check 4 — `workspace.yaml` integrity
55	
56	Verify:
57	- `model_policy` section exists with per-phase `allowed` + `default` entries for: `implementation`, `self_review`, `pr_description`, `suggested_next_step`.
58	- At least one repo is declared under `repos:`.
59	- `management_repo: management-repo` field exists at the top level.
60	- `repos[0].id` is exactly `management-repo` — the management repo must be the first entry.
61	- A repo entry with `id: management-repo` exists and has `github`, `local_path`, and `base_branch` set.
62	
63	For each issue found: report it and offer to fix it interactively.
64	
65	### Check 5 — Skill symlinks
66	
67	Execute all four repair steps in order. Do not skip any step even if things appear healthy.
68	
69	**Step 1 — Run repair-skills.sh.** This single command handles broken symlink removal, install.sh, and git untracking:
70	
71	```bash
72	<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/scripts/repair-skills.sh <current_directory>
73	```
74	
75	Do not reconstruct these steps manually.
76	
77	**Step 2 — Write `.claude/skills/.gitignore`.** Use the Write tool to create or overwrite the file unconditionally with exactly:
78	
79	```
80	# Shared skill symlinks are managed by workflow/scripts/install.sh — do not commit them.
81	# Symlinks are git blobs, caught by *. Real local skill directories are trees, preserved by !*/.
82	*
83	!*/
84	!.gitignore
85	```
86	
87	### Check 6 — `CLAUDE.md` shared section
88	
89	Use the Read tool to read `CLAUDE.md`. Check whether it contains the `BEGIN SHARED WORKFLOW RULES` / `END SHARED WORKFLOW RULES` marker block and whether the content between them matches `CLAUDE.shared.md` (also read with the Read tool).
90	
91	**Never use bash to check or compare this section.** The markers contain `<!--` which corrupts bash tool calls in Claude Code. Read both files with the Read tool and compare directly. If missing or stale, invoke `sync-workspace-rules`.
92	
93	### Verify & repair summary
94	
95	After all checks, print a status table:
96	
97	```
98	Check                        Status
99	---                          ---
100	.env file                    ✓ exists / ✗ created
101	Required vars complete       ✓ all set / ✗ fixed: GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, SSH_KEY_PATH
102	.env.template in sync        ✓ no missing keys / ✗ warned: <list of keys missing from .env>
103	workspace.yaml integrity     ✓ / ✗ <description of issue>
104	Skill symlinks               ✓ / ✗ repaired (N broken removed, N untracked)
105	  └─ .claude/skills/.gitignore  ✓ / ✗ created or repaired
106	CLAUDE.md shared section     ✓ / ✗ resynced
107	```
108	
109	If everything was already healthy, say so clearly. If repairs were made, list what changed.
110	
111	---
112	
113	## Mode B — Create (new workspace)
114	
115	Guide the user step-by-step through creating a new workspace using `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/templates/workspace/`.
116	
117	Do NOT create any files until all **Required** values have been collected. Walk the user through each phase in order, asking one group of questions at a time. Confirm values before writing.
118	
119	---
120	
121	## How required variables are determined
122	
123	Each workflow skill declares its own `## Environment` section listing the variables it needs. Before asking the user anything, read the `## Environment` section of every skill being installed (all files under `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/workflow/workflow_skills/*/SKILL.md`) and aggregate a unified required/deferrable list.
124	
125	The tables below reflect the current aggregated result. If new skills are added, re-aggregate before running the wizard.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## Required vs. Deferrable
130	
131	### Required — must be answered before any files are written
132	
133	Aggregated from all skill `## Environment` sections. These values are load-bearing.
134	
135	| Variable / Decision | Declared by | Why it's required |
136	|---|---|---|
137	| `WORKSPACE_ROOT` | `resolve-project-env`, `init-feature`, `sync-workspace-rules` | All skill path resolution depends on this |
138	| `WORKSPACE_ID` | `init-workspace` | Unique slug (used in file paths and YAML keys) |
139	| `WORKSPACE_NAME` | `init-workspace` | Human-readable project name |
140	| `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` | `resolve-project-env`, `pr-create`, `start-implementation` | Git identity for commits; used as approval actor |
141	| `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` | `resolve-project-env`, `approve-feature`, `reject-feature` | Directly referenced as `actor_source: env:GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` in workspace.yaml |
142	| `GITHUB_ACCOUNT` | `resolve-project-env`, `pr-create` | Required for PR creation and SSH remote URLs |
143	| `SSH_KEY_PATH` | `resolve-project-env`, `pr-create`, `start-implementation` | Required for SSH-based repo access; do not assume `~/.ssh/id_rsa` |
144	| Management repo (`repos[0].id: management-repo`) | `start-implementation`, `init-workspace` | The management repo stores all task state; must be first in repos[] with the fixed id |
145	| `management_repo: management-repo` in workspace.yaml | `start-implementation` | Explicit pointer so workflow skills can locate task state without array-position logic |
146	| `MANAGEMENT_REPO_LOCAL_PATH` | `resolve-project-env`, `start-implementation` | Local path to the management repo clone; required for claim commits |
147	| `<REPO_ID_UPPER>_LOCAL_PATH` per repo | `resolve-project-env`, `start-implementation` | Required for any git task execution |
148	| `base_branch` per repo | `start-implementation`, `pr-create` | Per-repo base branch declared in `workspace.yaml`; no global default |
149	| `model_policy` per-phase model allowlist | `init-workspace` | workspace.yaml centralized model cost control — defines allowed models + default per phase |
150	
151	### Deferrable — can be set later
152	
153	These can be left blank or use defaults; the user can fill them in before first feature.
154	
155	| Variable / Decision | Default | Notes |
156	|---|---|---|
157	| `SKIP_STAGING` | `false` | Change if project has no staging environment |
158	| Additional repo local paths | empty | Add as more repos are onboarded |
159	| Project purpose/description | placeholder | Fills the CLAUDE.md project context section |
160	| Non-standard environments | develop/staging/production | Remove or rename if the project differs |
161	| `infra_engineer` / `qa` actor type | `human` | Change to `agent` if automated |
162	
163	---
164	
165	## Mode B — Step-by-step guided flow (create)
166	
167	Walk through each phase in order. Ask, receive, confirm, then move to the next phase. Never skip a required phase.
168	
169	### Phase 1 — Workspace root
170	
171	1. Read `.env` in the current directory and look for `WORKSPACE_ROOT`.
172	2. If found, confirm it with the user: _"I found WORKSPACE_ROOT=<value>. Is this correct?"_
173	3. If missing, ask: _"What is your local workspace root path? (The folder that contains `workflow/` and your project folders)"_
174	4. Do not proceed until `WORKSPACE_ROOT` is confirmed.
175	
176	### Phase 2 — Project identity
177	
178	Ask all at once:
179	
180	- **Workspace ID** — a short lowercase slug (e.g. `my-project`). This becomes the folder name and YAML key.
181	- **Workspace name** — human-readable (e.g. `My Project`).
182	- **Project purpose** — one sentence describing what this project does. (Can say "I'll fill this in later.")
183	
184	Derive `WORKSPACE_ID` from the slug. Reject IDs with spaces or uppercase.
185	
186	### Phase 3 — Git identity
187	
188	1. Try to read git config: run `git config user.name` and `git config user.email`.
189	2. Show the result: _"Your git config has: name=<X>, email=<Y>. Use these?"_
190	3. If user says no, or values are empty, ask for them explicitly.
191	4. Both `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` and `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` are required.
192	
193	### Phase 4 — GitHub & SSH
194	
195	Ask:
196	
197	- **GitHub account** — the GitHub username or organization that owns the repos for this project. Example: `mycompany`.
198	- **SSH key path** — the path to the SSH private key to use for git operations. Suggest detecting existing keys: check if `~/.ssh/id_ed25519` or `~/.ssh/id_rsa` exist and offer them as options. The user must confirm or provide an explicit path. Do not assume.
199	
200	Both are required.
201	
202	### Phase 5 — Repository setup
203	
204	Every workspace requires a management repo as its first and mandatory repo entry. The management repo stores all task YAMLs, feature docs, and `CLAUDE.md` — it is the authoritative record of task state.
205	
206	**Step 5a — Management repo (always first, id is fixed)**
207	
208	The first repo is always the management repo. Its `id` is always `management-repo` — do not ask the user to choose an ID. Ask only:
209	
210	1. **GitHub URL** — SSH URL of the management repo, e.g. `git@github.com:mycompany/my-project.git`
211	2. **Local path value** — the filesystem path to the local clone (can be left blank for now)
212	3. **Base branch** — required, no default. Ask explicitly; do not assume `main`.
213	
214	Auto-derive the env var name as `MANAGEMENT_REPO_LOCAL_PATH`.
215	
216	After collecting, set `management_repo: management-repo` in `workspace.yaml` and write the entry as `repos[0]`.
217	
218	**Validation**: reject any workspace.yaml where `repos[0].id` is not `management-repo`. If a user tries to set a different ID for the first repo, explain the convention and correct it.
219	
220	**Step 5b — Implementation repos**
221	
222	Ask:
223	
224	_"How many implementation repositories does this project have? (You can add more later.)"_
225	
226	For each additional repo:
227	
228	1. **Repo ID** — short slug, e.g. `my-project-api`
229	2. **GitHub URL** — full SSH URL, e.g. `git@github.com:mycompany/my-project-api.git`
230	3. **Local path env var name** — auto-suggest based on repo ID in uppercase, e.g. `MY_PROJECT_API_LOCAL_PATH`
231	4. **Local path value** — the actual local filesystem path to the repo clone (can be left blank for now)
232	5. **Base branch** — required, no default. Ask explicitly.
233	6. **Owner role** — which role owns this repo: `backend_engineer`, `frontend_engineer`, or `data_engineer`
234	
235	Collect all repos before moving to the next phase.
236	
237	### Phase 6 — Model policy
238	
239	Explain to the user: _"The workspace defines which LLM models agents may use for each phase of task execution. This is the centralized cost-control surface."_
240	
241	Show the four phases and ask the user to confirm or customize:
242	- **implementation** — the main code-writing step (default: `claude-sonnet-4-6`)
243	- **self_review** — agent reviews its own diff (default: `claude-sonnet-4-6`)
244	- **pr_description** — summarize changes for the PR (default: `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`)
245	- **suggested_next_step** — brief explanation when blocked (default: `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`)
246	
247	For each phase, ask which models to allow and which to set as default. Accept the defaults if the user presses enter.
248	
249	This populates `model_policy` in `workspace.yaml`.
250	
251	### Phase 7 — Confirm and write
252	
253	Show the user a summary of all collected values:
254	
255	```
256	WORKSPACE_ROOT:      <value>
257	WORKSPACE_ID:        <value>
258	WORKSPACE_NAME:      <value>
259	GIT_AUTHOR_NAME:     <value>
260	GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL:    <value>
261	GITHUB_ACCOUNT:      <value>
262	SSH_KEY_PATH:        <value>
263	Repos:               <list with id, github, local_path, base_branch, owner_role>
264	model_policy:        <per-phase allowed + default>
265	```
266	
267	Ask: _"Everything look right? Type 'yes' to create the workspace, or let me know what to change."_
268	
269	Do not write any files until the user confirms.
270	
271	---
272	
273	## Mode B — Must create (after confirmation)
274	
275	- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/CLAUDE.md`
276	- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/workspace.yaml`
277	- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/.env.template`
278	- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/.env` (pre-filled with all confirmed values)
279	- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/docs/overview.md`
280	- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/docs/features/README.md`
281	- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/docs/features/.gitkeep`
282	- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/.claude/skills/.gitignore` (see content below)
283	
284	## Must use
285	
286	- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/workflow/CLAUDE.shared.md` — embed as the shared workflow section in `CLAUDE.md`
287	- Project-local context section (name + purpose) in `CLAUDE.md`
288	- Project-specific additional rules section in `CLAUDE.md`
289	
290	## `.claude/skills/.gitignore` content
291	
292	```
293	# Shared skill symlinks are managed by workflow/scripts/install.sh — do not commit them.
294	# Symlinks are git blobs, caught by *. Real local skill directories are trees, preserved by !*/.
295	*
296	!*/
297	!.gitignore
298	```
299	
300	This ensures shared symlinks are never committed while real workspace-specific skill directories remain tracked. New symlinks added by install.sh are automatically ignored — no manual updates required. Broken symlinks (target missing) are also ignored, but should be cleaned up before install.sh runs.
301	
302	## Must also invoke
303	
304	```bash
305	<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/workflow/scripts/install.sh <WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>
306	```
307	
308	---
309	
310	## Skill installation model
311	
312	- `<project>/.claude/skills/` must remain a real directory
313	- shared skills must be symlinked one by one inside it
314	- project-specific local skills must remain possible
315	
316	---
317	
318	## workspace.yaml — repos section
319	
320	The management repo is always first with the fixed id `management-repo`. Implementation repos follow.
321	
322	```yaml
323	management_repo: management-repo
324	
325	repos:
326	  # Management repo — always first, id is always management-repo
327	  - id: management-repo
328	    github: <github-url>
329	    local_path: env:MANAGEMENT_REPO_LOCAL_PATH
330	    base_branch: <base-branch>   # required; no default
331	
332	  # Implementation repos
333	  - id: <repo-id>
334	    github: <github-url>
335	    local_path: env:<LOCAL_PATH_ENV_VAR>
336	    base_branch: <base-branch>   # required; no default
337	    owner_role: <role>
338	```
339	
340	## workspace.yaml — model_policy
341	
342	```yaml
343	model_policy:
344	  implementation:
345	    allowed: [claude-sonnet-4-6]
346	    default: claude-sonnet-4-6
347	  self_review:
348	    allowed: [claude-sonnet-4-6]
349	    default: claude-sonnet-4-6
350	  pr_description:
351	    allowed: [claude-haiku-4-5-20251001]
352	    default: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
353	  suggested_next_step:
354	    allowed: [claude-haiku-4-5-20251001]
355	    default: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
356	```
357	
358	---
359	
360	## .env.template content
361	
362	The template should contain all variable names (no values), with comments grouping them:
363	
364	```env
365	# Workspace identity
366	WORKSPACE_ROOT=
367	WORKSPACE_ID=<workspace-id>
368	WORKSPACE_NAME=<workspace-name>
369	
370	# Git identity
371	GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=
372	GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=
373	
374	# GitHub
375	GITHUB_ACCOUNT=
376	SSH_KEY_PATH=~/.ssh/your_key_name
377	
378	# Workflow defaults
379	SKIP_STAGING=false
380	
381	# Repo local paths
382	<REPO_ENV_VAR>=
383	```
384	
385	The `.env` file should be pre-filled with all confirmed values.
386	
387	---
388	
389	## Rules
390	
391	**Both modes:**
392	- Detect mode from the filesystem — do not ask the user
393	- Do not duplicate shared skills into the project
394	- Do not symlink the entire `workflow_skills/` directory as one link
395	- `model_policy` is required in `workspace.yaml` — it defines which models agents may use per phase
396	- If `WORKSPACE_ROOT` cannot be determined, ask the user before proceeding
397	- Use `repair-skills.sh` as the single repair command for symlinks — do not reconstruct its steps manually
398	- After running `repair-skills.sh`, always write `.claude/skills/.gitignore` with the Write tool — unconditionally, do not check first
399	
400	**Create mode (Mode B) only:**
401	- Do NOT write any files until the user confirms the summary in Phase 7
402	- Do not leave the project in a half-installed state
403	- Remind the user at the end about any deferrable values left blank
404	
405	**Verify & repair mode (Mode A) only:**
406	- Fix missing required vars as they are found — do not batch them to the end
407	- Write each fixed value to `.env` immediately after the user provides it
408	- Do not prompt for deferrable variables unless the user explicitly asks
409	- Do not overwrite values already set in `.env` — only fill in empty or missing ones
410	- Always run `install.sh` regardless of whether symlinks appear healthy
411

file.content:
---
name: init-workspace
description: Initialize a new workspace OR verify and repair an existing one. Detects mode from the current directory — runs guided setup if no workspace is found, runs health checks and fixes if one already exists.
---

## Task

Two modes — detect which applies before doing anything else.

---

## Mode detection

Check whether the current directory is an existing workspace:

1. Does `./workspace.yaml` exist?
2. Does `./CLAUDE.md` exist?

| Condition | Mode |
|---|---|
| Both exist | **Verify & repair** — check and fix the existing workspace |
| Neither or only one exists | **Create** — run the guided setup wizard |

Do not ask the user which mode to use. Detect it from the filesystem.

---

## Mode A — Verify & repair (existing workspace)

When the current directory is an existing workspace, verify its health and fix anything missing or broken. Work through each check in order. Report a summary at the end.

### Check 1 — `.env` file exists

- If `.env` is missing and `.env.template` exists: offer to create `.env` from the template. Do so if the user agrees.
- If both are missing: create `.env` from scratch using the template structure.

### Check 2 — Required variables populated

Read `.env` and check each required variable from the table in **Required vs. Deferrable**. Also read `workspace.yaml` to discover which `<REPO_ID_UPPER>_LOCAL_PATH` variables are expected (one per repo in the `repos:` list).

For each missing or empty required variable:
- Tell the user which variable is missing and which skill requires it.
- Run the relevant phase from the guided wizard (Phase 3 for git identity, Phase 4 for GitHub/SSH, etc.) to collect the value.
- Write the value into `.env` immediately — do not batch; fix as you go.

Do not prompt for deferrable variables unless the user asks.

### Check 3 — `.env.template` in sync

Compare `.env.template` against `.env`. For any variable key present in `.env.template` but missing or empty in `.env`, warn the operator and prompt them to add it (following the same flow as Check 2).

Do **not** modify `.env.template` automatically. The template is the source of truth — the sync direction is `.env.template` → `.env`, never the reverse. Keys that exist in `.env` but are absent from `.env.template` are not a problem and require no action.

### Check 4 — `workspace.yaml` integrity

Verify:
- `model_policy` section exists with per-phase `allowed` + `default` entries for: `implementation`, `self_review`, `pr_description`, `suggested_next_step`.
- At least one repo is declared under `repos:`.
- `management_repo: management-repo` field exists at the top level.
- `repos[0].id` is exactly `management-repo` — the management repo must be the first entry.
- A repo entry with `id: management-repo` exists and has `github`, `local_path`, and `base_branch` set.

For each issue found: report it and offer to fix it interactively.

### Check 5 — Skill symlinks

Execute all four repair steps in order. Do not skip any step even if things appear healthy.

**Step 1 — Run repair-skills.sh.** This single command handles broken symlink removal, install.sh, and git untracking:

```bash
<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/scripts/repair-skills.sh <current_directory>
```

Do not reconstruct these steps manually.

**Step 2 — Write `.claude/skills/.gitignore`.** Use the Write tool to create or overwrite the file unconditionally with exactly:

```
# Shared skill symlinks are managed by workflow/scripts/install.sh — do not commit them.
# Symlinks are git blobs, caught by *. Real local skill directories are trees, preserved by !*/.
*
!*/
!.gitignore
```

### Check 6 — `CLAUDE.md` shared section

Use the Read tool to read `CLAUDE.md`. Check whether it contains the `BEGIN SHARED WORKFLOW RULES` / `END SHARED WORKFLOW RULES` marker block and whether the content between them matches `CLAUDE.shared.md` (also read with the Read tool).

**Never use bash to check or compare this section.** The markers contain `<!--` which corrupts bash tool calls in Claude Code. Read both files with the Read tool and compare directly. If missing or stale, invoke `sync-workspace-rules`.

### Verify & repair summary

After all checks, print a status table:

```
Check                        Status
---                          ---
.env file                    ✓ exists / ✗ created
Required vars complete       ✓ all set / ✗ fixed: GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, SSH_KEY_PATH
.env.template in sync        ✓ no missing keys / ✗ warned: <list of keys missing from .env>
workspace.yaml integrity     ✓ / ✗ <description of issue>
Skill symlinks               ✓ / ✗ repaired (N broken removed, N untracked)
  └─ .claude/skills/.gitignore  ✓ / ✗ created or repaired
CLAUDE.md shared section     ✓ / ✗ resynced
```

If everything was already healthy, say so clearly. If repairs were made, list what changed.

---

## Mode B — Create (new workspace)

Guide the user step-by-step through creating a new workspace using `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/templates/workspace/`.

Do NOT create any files until all **Required** values have been collected. Walk the user through each phase in order, asking one group of questions at a time. Confirm values before writing.

---

## How required variables are determined

Each workflow skill declares its own `## Environment` section listing the variables it needs. Before asking the user anything, read the `## Environment` section of every skill being installed (all files under `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/workflow/workflow_skills/*/SKILL.md`) and aggregate a unified required/deferrable list.

The tables below reflect the current aggregated result. If new skills are added, re-aggregate before running the wizard.

---

## Required vs. Deferrable

### Required — must be answered before any files are written

Aggregated from all skill `## Environment` sections. These values are load-bearing.

| Variable / Decision | Declared by | Why it's required |
|---|---|---|
| `WORKSPACE_ROOT` | `resolve-project-env`, `init-feature`, `sync-workspace-rules` | All skill path resolution depends on this |
| `WORKSPACE_ID` | `init-workspace` | Unique slug (used in file paths and YAML keys) |
| `WORKSPACE_NAME` | `init-workspace` | Human-readable project name |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` | `resolve-project-env`, `pr-create`, `start-implementation` | Git identity for commits; used as approval actor |
| `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` | `resolve-project-env`, `approve-feature`, `reject-feature` | Directly referenced as `actor_source: env:GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` in workspace.yaml |
| `GITHUB_ACCOUNT` | `resolve-project-env`, `pr-create` | Required for PR creation and SSH remote URLs |
| `SSH_KEY_PATH` | `resolve-project-env`, `pr-create`, `start-implementation` | Required for SSH-based repo access; do not assume `~/.ssh/id_rsa` |
| Management repo (`repos[0].id: management-repo`) | `start-implementation`, `init-workspace` | The management repo stores all task state; must be first in repos[] with the fixed id |
| `management_repo: management-repo` in workspace.yaml | `start-implementation` | Explicit pointer so workflow skills can locate task state without array-position logic |
| `MANAGEMENT_REPO_LOCAL_PATH` | `resolve-project-env`, `start-implementation` | Local path to the management repo clone; required for claim commits |
| `<REPO_ID_UPPER>_LOCAL_PATH` per repo | `resolve-project-env`, `start-implementation` | Required for any git task execution |
| `base_branch` per repo | `start-implementation`, `pr-create` | Per-repo base branch declared in `workspace.yaml`; no global default |
| `model_policy` per-phase model allowlist | `init-workspace` | workspace.yaml centralized model cost control — defines allowed models + default per phase |

### Deferrable — can be set later

These can be left blank or use defaults; the user can fill them in before first feature.

| Variable / Decision | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `SKIP_STAGING` | `false` | Change if project has no staging environment |
| Additional repo local paths | empty | Add as more repos are onboarded |
| Project purpose/description | placeholder | Fills the CLAUDE.md project context section |
| Non-standard environments | develop/staging/production | Remove or rename if the project differs |
| `infra_engineer` / `qa` actor type | `human` | Change to `agent` if automated |

---

## Mode B — Step-by-step guided flow (create)

Walk through each phase in order. Ask, receive, confirm, then move to the next phase. Never skip a required phase.

### Phase 1 — Workspace root

1. Read `.env` in the current directory and look for `WORKSPACE_ROOT`.
2. If found, confirm it with the user: _"I found WORKSPACE_ROOT=<value>. Is this correct?"_
3. If missing, ask: _"What is your local workspace root path? (The folder that contains `workflow/` and your project folders)"_
4. Do not proceed until `WORKSPACE_ROOT` is confirmed.

### Phase 2 — Project identity

Ask all at once:

- **Workspace ID** — a short lowercase slug (e.g. `my-project`). This becomes the folder name and YAML key.
- **Workspace name** — human-readable (e.g. `My Project`).
- **Project purpose** — one sentence describing what this project does. (Can say "I'll fill this in later.")

Derive `WORKSPACE_ID` from the slug. Reject IDs with spaces or uppercase.

### Phase 3 — Git identity

1. Try to read git config: run `git config user.name` and `git config user.email`.
2. Show the result: _"Your git config has: name=<X>, email=<Y>. Use these?"_
3. If user says no, or values are empty, ask for them explicitly.
4. Both `GIT_AUTHOR_NAME` and `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` are required.

### Phase 4 — GitHub & SSH

Ask:

- **GitHub account** — the GitHub username or organization that owns the repos for this project. Example: `mycompany`.
- **SSH key path** — the path to the SSH private key to use for git operations. Suggest detecting existing keys: check if `~/.ssh/id_ed25519` or `~/.ssh/id_rsa` exist and offer them as options. The user must confirm or provide an explicit path. Do not assume.

Both are required.

### Phase 5 — Repository setup

Every workspace requires a management repo as its first and mandatory repo entry. The management repo stores all task YAMLs, feature docs, and `CLAUDE.md` — it is the authoritative record of task state.

**Step 5a — Management repo (always first, id is fixed)**

The first repo is always the management repo. Its `id` is always `management-repo` — do not ask the user to choose an ID. Ask only:

1. **GitHub URL** — SSH URL of the management repo, e.g. `git@github.com:mycompany/my-project.git`
2. **Local path value** — the filesystem path to the local clone (can be left blank for now)
3. **Base branch** — required, no default. Ask explicitly; do not assume `main`.

Auto-derive the env var name as `MANAGEMENT_REPO_LOCAL_PATH`.

After collecting, set `management_repo: management-repo` in `workspace.yaml` and write the entry as `repos[0]`.

**Validation**: reject any workspace.yaml where `repos[0].id` is not `management-repo`. If a user tries to set a different ID for the first repo, explain the convention and correct it.

**Step 5b — Implementation repos**

Ask:

_"How many implementation repositories does this project have? (You can add more later.)"_

For each additional repo:

1. **Repo ID** — short slug, e.g. `my-project-api`
2. **GitHub URL** — full SSH URL, e.g. `git@github.com:mycompany/my-project-api.git`
3. **Local path env var name** — auto-suggest based on repo ID in uppercase, e.g. `MY_PROJECT_API_LOCAL_PATH`
4. **Local path value** — the actual local filesystem path to the repo clone (can be left blank for now)
5. **Base branch** — required, no default. Ask explicitly.
6. **Owner role** — which role owns this repo: `backend_engineer`, `frontend_engineer`, or `data_engineer`

Collect all repos before moving to the next phase.

### Phase 6 — Model policy

Explain to the user: _"The workspace defines which LLM models agents may use for each phase of task execution. This is the centralized cost-control surface."_

Show the four phases and ask the user to confirm or customize:
- **implementation** — the main code-writing step (default: `claude-sonnet-4-6`)
- **self_review** — agent reviews its own diff (default: `claude-sonnet-4-6`)
- **pr_description** — summarize changes for the PR (default: `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`)
- **suggested_next_step** — brief explanation when blocked (default: `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`)

For each phase, ask which models to allow and which to set as default. Accept the defaults if the user presses enter.

This populates `model_policy` in `workspace.yaml`.

### Phase 7 — Confirm and write

Show the user a summary of all collected values:

```
WORKSPACE_ROOT:      <value>
WORKSPACE_ID:        <value>
WORKSPACE_NAME:      <value>
GIT_AUTHOR_NAME:     <value>
GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL:    <value>
GITHUB_ACCOUNT:      <value>
SSH_KEY_PATH:        <value>
Repos:               <list with id, github, local_path, base_branch, owner_role>
model_policy:        <per-phase allowed + default>
```

Ask: _"Everything look right? Type 'yes' to create the workspace, or let me know what to change."_

Do not write any files until the user confirms.

---

## Mode B — Must create (after confirmation)

- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/CLAUDE.md`
- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/workspace.yaml`
- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/.env.template`
- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/.env` (pre-filled with all confirmed values)
- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/docs/overview.md`
- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/docs/features/README.md`
- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/docs/features/.gitkeep`
- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>/.claude/skills/.gitignore` (see content below)

## Must use

- `<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/workflow/CLAUDE.shared.md` — embed as the shared workflow section in `CLAUDE.md`
- Project-local context section (name + purpose) in `CLAUDE.md`
- Project-specific additional rules section in `CLAUDE.md`

## `.claude/skills/.gitignore` content

```
# Shared skill symlinks are managed by workflow/scripts/install.sh — do not commit them.
# Symlinks are git blobs, caught by *. Real local skill directories are trees, preserved by !*/.
*
!*/
!.gitignore
```

This ensures shared symlinks are never committed while real workspace-specific skill directories remain tracked. New symlinks added by install.sh are automatically ignored — no manual updates required. Broken symlinks (target missing) are also ignored, but should be cleaned up before install.sh runs.

## Must also invoke

```bash
<WORKSPACE_ROOT>/workflow/scripts/install.sh <WORKSPACE_ROOT>/<WORKSPACE_ID>
```

---

## Skill installation model

- `<project>/.claude/skills/` must remain a real directory
- shared skills must be symlinked one by one inside it
- project-specific local skills must remain possible

---

## workspace.yaml — repos section

The management repo is always first with the fixed id `management-repo`. Implementation repos follow.

```yaml
management_repo: management-repo

repos:
  # Management repo — always first, id is always management-repo
  - id: management-repo
    github: <github-url>
    local_path: env:MANAGEMENT_REPO_LOCAL_PATH
    base_branch: <base-branch>   # required; no default

  # Implementation repos
  - id: <repo-id>
    github: <github-url>
    local_path: env:<LOCAL_PATH_ENV_VAR>
    base_branch: <base-branch>   # required; no default
    owner_role: <role>
```

## workspace.yaml — model_policy

```yaml
model_policy:
  implementation:
    allowed: [claude-sonnet-4-6]
    default: claude-sonnet-4-6
  self_review:
    allowed: [claude-sonnet-4-6]
    default: claude-sonnet-4-6
  pr_description:
    allowed: [claude-haiku-4-5-20251001]
    default: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
  suggested_next_step:
    allowed: [claude-haiku-4-5-20251001]
    default: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
```

---

## .env.template content

The template should contain all variable names (no values), with comments grouping them:

```env
# Workspace identity
WORKSPACE_ROOT=
WORKSPACE_ID=<workspace-id>
WORKSPACE_NAME=<workspace-name>

# Git identity
GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=
GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=

# GitHub
GITHUB_ACCOUNT=
SSH_KEY_PATH=~/.ssh/your_key_name

# Workflow defaults
SKIP_STAGING=false

# Repo local paths
<REPO_ENV_VAR>=
```

The `.env` file should be pre-filled with all confirmed values.

---

## Rules

**Both modes:**
- Detect mode from the filesystem — do not ask the user
- Do not duplicate shared skills into the project
- Do not symlink the entire `workflow_skills/` directory as one link
- `model_policy` is required in `workspace.yaml` — it defines which models agents may use per phase
- If `WORKSPACE_ROOT` cannot be determined, ask the user before proceeding
- Use `repair-skills.sh` as the single repair command for symlinks — do not reconstruct its steps manually
- After running `repair-skills.sh`, always write `.claude/skills/.gitignore` with the Write tool — unconditionally, do not check first

**Create mode (Mode B) only:**
- Do NOT write any files until the user confirms the summary in Phase 7
- Do not leave the project in a half-installed state
- Remind the user at the end about any deferrable values left blank

**Verify & repair mode (Mode A) only:**
- Fix missing required vars as they are found — do not batch them to the end
- Write each fixed value to `.env` immediately after the user provides it
- Do not prompt for deferrable variables unless the user explicitly asks
- Do not overwrite values already set in `.env` — only fill in empty or missing ones
- Always run `install.sh` regardless of whether symlinks appear healthy

1	# Agent-runtime local quickstart
2	
3	Run two agents locally against your workspace in under 10 minutes.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Docker 24+ with Buildx (`docker buildx version`)
8	- Docker Compose v2 (`docker compose version`)
9	- An SSH key with read/write access to your workspace repo(s)
10	- An Anthropic API key
11	- A GitHub personal access token (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) with `repo` scope — needed by the `pr-create` skill
12	
13	## 1. Configure
14	
15	```bash
16	cd agent-runtime/orchestration/local
17	
18	# Create the env file
19	cp .env.example .env
20	# Edit .env — at minimum set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL
21	
22	# Create your agent config
23	cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml
24	# Edit agent.yaml — set watches: to your workspace repo SSH URL
25	```
26	
27	**Minimal `agent.yaml`:**
28	
29	```yaml
30	watches:
31	  - git@github.com:your-org/your-workspace.git
32	enabled: true
33	jitter_max_seconds: 2
34	budget:
35	  max_tokens_per_task: 100000
36	  max_iterations: 30
37	log_sink:
38	  enabled: true
39	# Seconds to sleep between poll cycles. Set to 0 for single-shot mode.
40	idle_sleep_seconds: 60
41	```
42	
43	**Minimal `.env`:**
44	
45	```env
46	ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
47	GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...        # GitHub PAT with repo scope — required by pr-create skill
48	GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=you@example.com
49	GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Agent Bot
50	
51	# SSH URL of the workflow repo — cloned into /agent/workflow/ at startup.
52	# The agent uses this to resolve technical_skills/ at runtime.
53	WORKFLOW_URL=git@github.com:your-org/your-workflow.git
54	
55	# SSH key — paste the raw PEM content (preferred):
56	SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
57	...
58	-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
59	
60	# Or point to a key file inside the container (file-mount mode):
61	# SSH_KEY_DIR=~/.ssh
62	```
63	
64	## 2. Run agents
65	
66	From **`agent-runtime/orchestration/local/`**:
67	
68	```bash
69	docker compose up --build
70	```
71	
72	Or from the **`workflow/` root**:
73	
74	```bash
75	docker compose -f agent-runtime/orchestration/local/docker-compose.yml up --build
76	```
77	
78	`--build` is required on first run (or after code changes). On subsequent runs with no code changes, you can drop it.
79	
80	Both agents share the same workspace clone volume. Each container runs a continuous internal polling loop — repos are cloned once at startup, then pulled on every cycle.
81	
82	Each poll cycle:
83	1. Pull latest state — `git fetch + reset` on each watched workspace and its implementation repos.
84	2. Scan for `ready` tasks whose dependencies are met.
85	3. Race to claim a task via git push (SHA-based contention — one wins, one retries).
86	4. Run the claimed task through the Anthropic tool-use loop.
87	5. Sleep `idle_sleep_seconds` (default: 60 s), then repeat.
88	
89	`restart: unless-stopped` is still set for crash recovery, but is no longer the driver of the poll cadence. To run exactly one cycle and exit, set `idle_sleep_seconds: 0` in `agent.yaml`.
90	
91	**To enable a second agent:** uncomment the `agent-2` block in `docker-compose.yml`, then re-run `docker compose up --build`.
92	
93	## 3. Watch the output
94	
95	Events are emitted as JSON lines to stdout. Useful patterns to watch:
96	
97	```bash
98	# Follow all agents, pretty-print JSON
99	docker compose logs -f \
100	  | jq -R 'try fromjson catch .'
101	
102	# See only claim events
103	docker compose logs | grep '"type":"task_claimed\|claim_lost"'
104	```
105	
106	Key event types:
107	
108	| Event | Meaning |
109	|---|---|
110	| `bootstrap_started` | Agent pulled repos, config valid |
111	| `skill_reference_audit` | A skill slug in `tasks.md` has no matching `technical_skills/` dir |
112	| `poll_workspace_pulled` | Workspace git-pulled at start of cycle |
113	| `poll_git_halt` | Non-transient pull failure (auth/not-found/non-ff); workspace skipped this cycle |
114	| `poll_git_warn` | Transient pull failure (network); workspace skipped, retried next cycle |
115	| `task_claimed` | This agent won the claim race |
116	| `claim_lost` | Another agent claimed first; this agent idles |
117	| `no_eligible_tasks` | No ready tasks found (all done, blocked, or skill-missing) |
118	| `task_run_complete` | Task finished (check `outcome` field) |
119	| `activation_idle` | Nothing to do this cycle; sleeping `idle_sleep_seconds` |
120	| `activation_complete` | Task ran this cycle; sleeping `idle_sleep_seconds` before next cycle |
121	
122	## 4. Rebuild after code changes
123	
124	```bash
125	docker compose up --build
126	```
127	
128	## 5. Stop and reset
129	
130	Stop the agents:
131	
132	```bash
133	docker compose down
134	```
135	
136	The `workspaces` Docker volume persists between runs (so re-cloning is fast). To force a full re-clone:
137	
138	```bash
139	docker compose down -v
140	```
141	
142	## Other deployment targets
143	
144	The `local/` compose is for local development. For production or CI use one of:
145	
146	| Target | Path |
147	|---|---|
148	| Kubernetes Deployment | `orchestration/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
149	| GitHub Actions | `orchestration/github-actions/` |
150	
151	## Troubleshooting
152	
153	**`bootstrap_failed` with `reason: agent_yaml_invalid`**
154	Check your `agent.yaml` against `agent.yaml.example` — a missing field (e.g. `jitter_max_seconds`) will cause exit code 2.
155	
156	**`pr-create` skill fails or PRs are not created**
157	The `pr-create` skill requires a GitHub personal access token with `repo` scope.
158	Verify:
159	- `GITHUB_TOKEN` is set in `.env` (not empty)
160	- The token has `repo` scope — the built-in GHA `GITHUB_TOKEN` may lack write access to your target repo; use a dedicated PAT
161	- The token has not expired
162	
163	**`bootstrap_failed` with `reason: git_workspace_sync_failed`**
164	The SSH key can't reach your workspace repo. Verify:
165	- `SSH_PRIVATE_KEY` in `.env` contains the full PEM key (including `-----BEGIN` / `-----END` lines)
166	- Or `SSH_KEY_DIR` points to a directory containing `id_rsa` (file-mount mode)
167	- The key is added to your GitHub account
168	- The URL in `agent.yaml` `watches:` is an SSH URL (`git@github.com:...`)
169	
170	**Both agents show `no_eligible_tasks`**
171	All tasks in your workspace are either `todo` (deps not met), `in_progress`, `done`, or blocked on a missing skill. Check `list-features` in your workspace for current state.
172	
173	**`skill_reference_audit` warnings**
174	A `### Required skills` entry in `tasks.md` doesn't match any directory under `workflow/technical_skills/`. The agent skips that task (non-fatal). Fix the slug or create the skill directory.
175	
176	**Go 1.25 download fails during build**
177	Go 1.25 may not yet be published. Override: `--build-arg GO_VERSION=1.24.2`.
178	
179	See [DOCKER.md](./DOCKER.md) for the full environment variable reference.

file.content:
# Agent-runtime local quickstart

Run two agents locally against your workspace in under 10 minutes.

## Prerequisites

- Docker 24+ with Buildx (`docker buildx version`)
- Docker Compose v2 (`docker compose version`)
- An SSH key with read/write access to your workspace repo(s)
- An Anthropic API key
- A GitHub personal access token (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) with `repo` scope — needed by the `pr-create` skill

## 1. Configure

```bash
cd agent-runtime/orchestration/local

# Create the env file
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env — at minimum set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL

# Create your agent config
cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml
# Edit agent.yaml — set watches: to your workspace repo SSH URL
```

**Minimal `agent.yaml`:**

```yaml
watches:
  - git@github.com:your-org/your-workspace.git
enabled: true
jitter_max_seconds: 2
budget:
  max_tokens_per_task: 100000
  max_iterations: 30
log_sink:
  enabled: true
# Seconds to sleep between poll cycles. Set to 0 for single-shot mode.
idle_sleep_seconds: 60
```

**Minimal `.env`:**

```env
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...        # GitHub PAT with repo scope — required by pr-create skill
GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=you@example.com
GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Agent Bot

# SSH URL of the workflow repo — cloned into /agent/workflow/ at startup.
# The agent uses this to resolve technical_skills/ at runtime.
WORKFLOW_URL=git@github.com:your-org/your-workflow.git

# SSH key — paste the raw PEM content (preferred):
SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----

# Or point to a key file inside the container (file-mount mode):
# SSH_KEY_DIR=~/.ssh
```

## 2. Run agents

From **`agent-runtime/orchestration/local/`**:

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

Or from the **`workflow/` root**:

```bash
docker compose -f agent-runtime/orchestration/local/docker-compose.yml up --build
```

`--build` is required on first run (or after code changes). On subsequent runs with no code changes, you can drop it.

Both agents share the same workspace clone volume. Each container runs a continuous internal polling loop — repos are cloned once at startup, then pulled on every cycle.

Each poll cycle:
1. Pull latest state — `git fetch + reset` on each watched workspace and its implementation repos.
2. Scan for `ready` tasks whose dependencies are met.
3. Race to claim a task via git push (SHA-based contention — one wins, one retries).
4. Run the claimed task through the Anthropic tool-use loop.
5. Sleep `idle_sleep_seconds` (default: 60 s), then repeat.

`restart: unless-stopped` is still set for crash recovery, but is no longer the driver of the poll cadence. To run exactly one cycle and exit, set `idle_sleep_seconds: 0` in `agent.yaml`.

**To enable a second agent:** uncomment the `agent-2` block in `docker-compose.yml`, then re-run `docker compose up --build`.

## 3. Watch the output

Events are emitted as JSON lines to stdout. Useful patterns to watch:

```bash
# Follow all agents, pretty-print JSON
docker compose logs -f \
  | jq -R 'try fromjson catch .'

# See only claim events
docker compose logs | grep '"type":"task_claimed\|claim_lost"'
```

Key event types:

| Event | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `bootstrap_started` | Agent pulled repos, config valid |
| `skill_reference_audit` | A skill slug in `tasks.md` has no matching `technical_skills/` dir |
| `poll_workspace_pulled` | Workspace git-pulled at start of cycle |
| `poll_git_halt` | Non-transient pull failure (auth/not-found/non-ff); workspace skipped this cycle |
| `poll_git_warn` | Transient pull failure (network); workspace skipped, retried next cycle |
| `task_claimed` | This agent won the claim race |
| `claim_lost` | Another agent claimed first; this agent idles |
| `no_eligible_tasks` | No ready tasks found (all done, blocked, or skill-missing) |
| `task_run_complete` | Task finished (check `outcome` field) |
| `activation_idle` | Nothing to do this cycle; sleeping `idle_sleep_seconds` |
| `activation_complete` | Task ran this cycle; sleeping `idle_sleep_seconds` before next cycle |

## 4. Rebuild after code changes

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

## 5. Stop and reset

Stop the agents:

```bash
docker compose down
```

The `workspaces` Docker volume persists between runs (so re-cloning is fast). To force a full re-clone:

```bash
docker compose down -v
```

## Other deployment targets

The `local/` compose is for local development. For production or CI use one of:

| Target | Path |
|---|---|
| Kubernetes Deployment | `orchestration/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` |
| GitHub Actions | `orchestration/github-actions/` |

## Troubleshooting

**`bootstrap_failed` with `reason: agent_yaml_invalid`**
Check your `agent.yaml` against `agent.yaml.example` — a missing field (e.g. `jitter_max_seconds`) will cause exit code 2.

**`pr-create` skill fails or PRs are not created**
The `pr-create` skill requires a GitHub personal access token with `repo` scope.
Verify:
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` is set in `.env` (not empty)
- The token has `repo` scope — the built-in GHA `GITHUB_TOKEN` may lack write access to your target repo; use a dedicated PAT
- The token has not expired

**`bootstrap_failed` with `reason: git_workspace_sync_failed`**
The SSH key can't reach your workspace repo. Verify:
- `SSH_PRIVATE_KEY` in `.env` contains the full PEM key (including `-----BEGIN` / `-----END` lines)
- Or `SSH_KEY_DIR` points to a directory containing `id_rsa` (file-mount mode)
- The key is added to your GitHub account
- The URL in `agent.yaml` `watches:` is an SSH URL (`git@github.com:...`)

**Both agents show `no_eligible_tasks`**
All tasks in your workspace are either `todo` (deps not met), `in_progress`, `done`, or blocked on a missing skill. Check `list-features` in your workspace for current state.

**`skill_reference_audit` warnings**
A `### Required skills` entry in `tasks.md` doesn't match any directory under `workflow/technical_skills/`. The agent skips that task (non-fatal). Fix the slug or create the skill directory.

**Go 1.25 download fails during build**
Go 1.25 may not yet be published. Override: `--build-arg GO_VERSION=1.24.2`.

See [DOCKER.md](./DOCKER.md) for the full environment variable reference.

[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=42", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "rag-service"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "RAG services for agent context (schema, indexer, MCP server)"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dependencies = [
    "qdrant-client>=1.9.0",
    "sentence-transformers>=3.0.0",
    "fastapi>=0.115.0",
    "uvicorn[standard]>=0.30.0",
    "mcp>=1.0.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
test = [
    "pytest>=8.0",
    "pytest-cov>=5.0",
]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
python_files = ["test_*.py"]
python_classes = ["Test*"]
python_functions = ["test_*"]

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
where = ["."]
include = ["services*"]

file.content:
# Agent configuration file.
#
# Each agent host reads this file at container start to determine what work
# it is allowed to pick up and how much budget it may spend per task.
#
# This file intentionally omits agent identity, role, skills, and model
# selection. Attribution is derived from GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL in the host's
# .env file. Model selection is driven by workspace.yaml model_policy
# and optional per-task ### Model overrides in tasks.md.

# Workspace repositories this agent watches for eligible tasks.
# Each entry is an SSH URL to a workspace repo (not an implementation repo).
# The agent clones/pulls these at every activation cycle and scans for
# tasks whose repo is reachable from the watched workspace.
watches:
  - git@github.com:mycompany/workspace.git

# Kill switch. When false, the agent exits 0 immediately after bootstrap
# without scanning for work. Useful for draining an agent host gracefully.
enabled: true

# Maximum random jitter (in seconds) applied before a claim commit.
# De-synchronises agents that fire from the same cron tick to reduce
# push-rejection contention. Must be a non-negative integer.
jitter_max_seconds: 15

# Hard budget limits enforced per task activation.
budget:
  # Maximum tokens (input + output) the agent may consume on a single task.
  # Positive integer. When exceeded between iterations, the agent aborts
  # with blocked_reason: budget_exceeded.
  max_tokens_per_task: 200000

  # Maximum tool-use loop iterations per task. Positive integer.
  # When reached, the agent aborts with blocked_reason: iteration_cap_exceeded.
  max_iterations: 3

# Per-run log sink configuration.
log_sink:
  # Whether to write a JSONL event log for each task run.
  # Path is deterministic: docs/<feature>/logs/T<n>_<ISO-start>.jsonl
  # on the task's feature branch.
  enabled: true

input.old_string:
# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services. Each runs a continuous internal polling loop,
# pulling workspace state and scanning for ready tasks on every cycle.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.
#
# restart: unless-stopped provides crash recovery only — the poll cadence is
# controlled by idle_sleep_seconds in agent.yaml (default: 60 s), not by
# container restarts.
#
# Usage (from this directory):
#   cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml   # fill in watches: with your workspace SSH URL
#   cp .env.example .env               # fill in required values
#   docker compose up --build          # start agent-1 (foreground)
#   docker compose up --build -d       # start in background
#
# To add a second agent: uncomment the agent-2 block below, then re-run docker compose up.

name: agent-runtime-local

# ── Shared agent definition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
x-agent: &agent-base
  build:
    # Build context is workflow/ root so schemas/ is included.
    # Path is three levels up: local/ → orchestration/ → agent-runtime/ → workflow/
    context: ../../..
    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
    WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
    # Required for the pr-create skill (GitHub REST API).
    GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    # SSH key delivery: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (env var, preferred) or SSH_KEY_PATH (file mount).
    SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
  volumes:
    - ./agent.yaml:/agent/agent.yaml:ro
    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro
  restart: unless-stopped
  deploy:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpus: "4"
        memory: 10G
      reservations:
        cpus: "1"
        memory: 2G

# ── Services ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
services:
  agent-1:
    <<: *agent-base
    environment:
      AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
      WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
      WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
      GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
      SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
      SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
      GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 1"
      GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"

  # agent-2:
  #   <<: *agent-base
  #   environment:
  #     AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
  #     WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
  #     WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
  #     GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
  #     SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
  #     SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 2"
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"

# ── Volumes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
volumes:
  workspaces:
    driver: local
  workflow:
    driver: local
input.new_string:
# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services. Each runs a continuous internal polling loop,
# pulling workspace state and scanning for ready tasks on every cycle.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.
#
# restart: unless-stopped provides crash recovery only — the poll cadence is
# controlled by idle_sleep_seconds in agent.yaml (default: 60 s), not by
# container restarts.
#
# RAG stack (qdrant + rag-server + indexer) is optional — agents degrade
# gracefully when MCP_RAG_URL is unset. To enable:
#   1. Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH in .env to the path of your local rag-service clone.
#   2. Set WORKSPACE_ID in .env (e.g. "workspace").
#   3. Set REPO_PATHS in .env to comma-separated paths of repos to index
#      (e.g. "/agent/data,/agent/workflow").
#   4. docker compose --profile rag up --build
#
# Usage (from this directory):
#   cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml   # fill in watches: with your workspace SSH URL
#   cp .env.example .env               # fill in required values
#   docker compose up --build          # start agents only (no RAG)
#   docker compose --profile rag up --build  # start agents + RAG stack
#   docker compose up --build -d       # start in background

name: agent-runtime-local

# ── Shared agent definition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
x-agent: &agent-base
  build:
    # Build context is workflow/ root so schemas/ is included.
    # Path is three levels up: local/ → orchestration/ → agent-runtime/ → workflow/
    context: ../../..
    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
    WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
    # Required for the pr-create skill (GitHub REST API).
    GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    # SSH key delivery: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (env var, preferred) or SSH_KEY_PATH (file mount).
    SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
    # RAG context injection — optional. When set, the agent queries the RAG server
    # for relevant project context before starting each task. Defaults to the
    # rag-server companion service URL. Unset or empty = graceful cold start.
    MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-http://rag-server:8000}"
  volumes:
    - ./agent.yaml:/agent/agent.yaml:ro
    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro
  restart: unless-stopped
  deploy:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpus: "4"
        memory: 10G
      reservations:
        cpus: "1"
        memory: 2G

# ── Shared RAG build context ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Both rag-server and indexer build from the rag-service repo.
# RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH must point to your local clone of the rag-service repo.
x-rag-build: &rag-build
  context: ${RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH:?Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH to the local path of your rag-service clone (required for --profile rag)}
  dockerfile_inline: |
    FROM python:3.12-slim
    WORKDIR /app
    RUN pip install --no-cache-dir uv
    COPY requirements.txt .
    RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache -r requirements.txt
    COPY . .
    RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache -e .

# ── Services ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
services:
  agent-1:
    <<: *agent-base
    environment:
      AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
      WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
      WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
      GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
      SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
      SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
      GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 1"
      GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"
      MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-http://rag-server:8000}"

  # agent-2:
  #   <<: *agent-base
  #   environment:
  #     AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
  #     WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
  #     WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
  #     GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
  #     SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
  #     SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 2"
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"
  #     MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-http://rag-server:8000}"

  # ── RAG stack (optional — start with: docker compose --profile rag up) ────────
  qdrant:
    image: qdrant/qdrant:latest
    profiles: [rag]
    ports:
      - "6333:6333"
      - "6334:6334"
    volumes:
      - qdrant_data:/qdrant/storage
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 2G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.25"
          memory: 256M

  rag-server:
    build: *rag-build
    profiles: [rag]
    command: ["python", "-m", "services.rag_server.main"]
    depends_on:
      - qdrant
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      QDRANT_URL: "${QDRANT_URL:-http://qdrant:6333}"
      WORKSPACE_ID: "${WORKSPACE_ID:?WORKSPACE_ID must be set — e.g. 'workspace'}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.5"
          memory: 1G

  indexer:
    build: *rag-build
    profiles: [rag]
    command: ["python", "-m", "services.indexer.main"]
    depends_on:
      - qdrant
    environment:
      QDRANT_URL: "${QDRANT_URL:-http://qdrant:6333}"
      WORKSPACE_ID: "${WORKSPACE_ID:?WORKSPACE_ID must be set — e.g. 'workspace'}"
      INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS: "${INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS:-300}"
      # Comma-separated paths of repos to index inside the container.
      # Mount the repos you want indexed (e.g. management repo + workflow repo).
      # Example: REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspace,/repos/workflow
      REPO_PATHS: "${REPO_PATHS:?REPO_PATHS must be set — comma-separated list of repo paths to index}"
    volumes:
      - workspaces:/repos/workspaces:ro
      - workflow:/repos/workflow:ro
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.5"
          memory: 1G

# ── Volumes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
volumes:
  workspaces:
    driver: local
  workflow:
    driver: local
  qdrant_data:
    driver: local

# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services. Each runs a continuous internal polling loop,
# pulling workspace state and scanning for ready tasks on every cycle.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.
#
# restart: unless-stopped provides crash recovery only — the poll cadence is
# controlled by idle_sleep_seconds in agent.yaml (default: 60 s), not by
# container restarts.
#
# Usage (from this directory):
#   cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml   # fill in watches: with your workspace SSH URL
#   cp .env.example .env               # fill in required values
#   docker compose up --build          # start agent-1 (foreground)
#   docker compose up --build -d       # start in background
#
# To add a second agent: uncomment the agent-2 block below, then re-run docker compose up.

name: agent-runtime-local

# ── Shared agent definition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
x-agent: &agent-base
  build:
    # Build context is workflow/ root so schemas/ is included.
    # Path is three levels up: local/ → orchestration/ → agent-runtime/ → workflow/
    context: ../../..
    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
    WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
    # Required for the pr-create skill (GitHub REST API).
    GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    # SSH key delivery: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (env var, preferred) or SSH_KEY_PATH (file mount).
    SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
  volumes:
    - ./agent.yaml:/agent/agent.yaml:ro
    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro
  restart: unless-stopped
  deploy:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpus: "4"
        memory: 10G
      reservations:
        cpus: "1"
        memory: 2G

# ── Services ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
services:
  agent-1:

    environment:
      AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
      WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
      WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data

      SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"

  #     GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"

tool_use_result.newString:
# Agent-runtime — local multi-agent compose
#
# Two long-running agent services. Each runs a continuous internal polling loop,
# pulling workspace state and scanning for ready tasks on every cycle.
# Agent-2 is commented out by default; uncomment to run two agents in parallel.
#
# restart: unless-stopped provides crash recovery only — the poll cadence is
# controlled by idle_sleep_seconds in agent.yaml (default: 60 s), not by
# container restarts.
#
# RAG stack (qdrant + rag-server + indexer) is optional — agents degrade
# gracefully when MCP_RAG_URL is unset. To enable:
#   1. Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH in .env to the path of your local rag-service clone.
#   2. Set WORKSPACE_ID in .env (e.g. "workspace").
#   3. Set REPO_PATHS in .env to comma-separated paths of repos to index
#      (e.g. "/agent/data,/agent/workflow").
#   4. docker compose --profile rag up --build
#
# Usage (from this directory):
#   cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml   # fill in watches: with your workspace SSH URL
#   cp .env.example .env               # fill in required values
#   docker compose up --build          # start agents only (no RAG)
#   docker compose --profile rag up --build  # start agents + RAG stack
#   docker compose up --build -d       # start in background

name: agent-runtime-local

# ── Shared agent definition ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
x-agent: &agent-base
  build:
    # Build context is workflow/ root so schemas/ is included.
    # Path is three levels up: local/ → orchestration/ → agent-runtime/ → workflow/
    context: ../../..
    dockerfile: agent-runtime/Dockerfile
  env_file: .env
  environment:
    AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
    WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
    WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
    # Required for the pr-create skill (GitHub REST API).
    GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    # SSH key delivery: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY (env var, preferred) or SSH_KEY_PATH (file mount).
    SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
    # RAG context injection — optional. When set, the agent queries the RAG server
    # for relevant project context before starting each task. Defaults to the
    # rag-server companion service URL. Unset or empty = graceful cold start.
    MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-http://rag-server:8000}"
  volumes:
    - ./agent.yaml:/agent/agent.yaml:ro
    - workspaces:/agent/data
    - workflow:/agent/workflow
    - ${SSH_KEY_DIR:-${HOME}/.ssh}:/agent/ssh:ro
  restart: unless-stopped
  deploy:
    resources:
      limits:
        cpus: "4"
        memory: 10G
      reservations:
        cpus: "1"
        memory: 2G

# ── Shared RAG build context ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Both rag-server and indexer build from the rag-service repo.
# RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH must point to your local clone of the rag-service repo.
x-rag-build: &rag-build
  context: ${RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH:?Set RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH to the local path of your rag-service clone (required for --profile rag)}
  dockerfile_inline: |
    FROM python:3.12-slim
    WORKDIR /app
    RUN pip install --no-cache-dir uv
    COPY requirements.txt .
    RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache -r requirements.txt
    COPY . .
    RUN uv pip install --system --no-cache -e .

# ── Services ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
services:
  agent-1:
    <<: *agent-base
    environment:
      AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
      WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
      WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
      GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
      SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
      SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
      GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 1"
      GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"
      MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-http://rag-server:8000}"

  # agent-2:
  #   <<: *agent-base
  #   environment:
  #     AGENT_YAML_PATH: /agent/agent.yaml
  #     WORKFLOW_LOCAL_PATH: /agent/workflow
  #     WORKSPACES_ROOT: /agent/data
  #     GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
  #     SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: "${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
  #     SSH_KEY_PATH: /agent/ssh/id_rsa
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: "Agent 2"
  #     GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: "${GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL}"
  #     MCP_RAG_URL: "${MCP_RAG_URL:-http://rag-server:8000}"

  # ── RAG stack (optional — start with: docker compose --profile rag up) ────────
  qdrant:
    image: qdrant/qdrant:latest
    profiles: [rag]
    ports:
      - "6333:6333"
      - "6334:6334"
    volumes:
      - qdrant_data:/qdrant/storage
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 2G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.25"
          memory: 256M

  rag-server:
    build: *rag-build
    profiles: [rag]
    command: ["python", "-m", "services.rag_server.main"]
    depends_on:
      - qdrant
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      QDRANT_URL: "${QDRANT_URL:-http://qdrant:6333}"
      WORKSPACE_ID: "${WORKSPACE_ID:?WORKSPACE_ID must be set — e.g. 'workspace'}"
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.5"
          memory: 1G

  indexer:
    build: *rag-build
    profiles: [rag]
    command: ["python", "-m", "services.indexer.main"]
    depends_on:
      - qdrant
    environment:
      QDRANT_URL: "${QDRANT_URL:-http://qdrant:6333}"
      WORKSPACE_ID: "${WORKSPACE_ID:?WORKSPACE_ID must be set — e.g. 'workspace'}"
      INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS: "${INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS:-300}"
      # Comma-separated paths of repos to index inside the container.
      # Mount the repos you want indexed (e.g. management repo + workflow repo).
      # Example: REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspace,/repos/workflow
      REPO_PATHS: "${REPO_PATHS:?REPO_PATHS must be set — comma-separated list of repo paths to index}"
    volumes:
      - workspaces:/repos/workspaces:ro
      - workflow:/repos/workflow:ro
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: "2"
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          cpus: "0.5"
          memory: 1G

# ── Volumes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
volumes:
  workspaces:
    driver: local
  workflow:
    driver: local
  qdrant_data:
    driver: local

input.old_string:
# Copy this file to .env and fill in your values.
# cp .env.example .env

# ── Required ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Anthropic API key — get one from console.anthropic.com
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# GitHub personal access token with `repo` scope.
# Required by the pr-create skill to open pull requests via the GitHub REST API.
# Create at: https://github.com/settings/tokens
GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...

# Git identity written into task log entries and claim commits.
# Both agents use the same email (the claim protocol uses SHA not identity).
GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=you@example.com
GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Agent Bot

# ── Optional ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# SSH key delivery — choose one method:
#
# Option A (preferred for K8s / CI / GitHub Actions):
#   Paste the raw PEM content of your private key. The runtime writes it to a
#   temp file (0400) at startup. No volume mount needed.
# SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
#   ...key content...
# -----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
#
# Option B (Docker Compose / local with ~/.ssh volume mount):
#   Point to the key file path inside the container.
# SSH_KEY_DIR=~/.ssh   # mounts as /agent/ssh — used with SSH_KEY_PATH=/agent/ssh/id_rsa

# SSH URL of the workflow repo — cloned into /agent/workflow/ on each run.
# Required so the agent can resolve technical_skills/ at runtime.
WORKFLOW_URL=git@github.com:your-org/your-workflow.git

# Go version override — use if 1.25.0 is not yet available on go.dev/dl.
# GO_VERSION=1.24.2
input.new_string:

# ── Required ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Anthropic API key — get one from console.anthropic.com
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# GitHub personal access token with `repo` scope.
# Required by the pr-create skill to open pull requests via the GitHub REST API.
# Create at: https://github.com/settings/tokens
GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...

# Git identity written into task log entries and claim commits.
# Both agents use the same email (the claim protocol uses SHA not identity).
GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=you@example.com
GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Agent Bot

# ── Optional — SSH ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# SSH key delivery — choose one method:
#
# Option A (preferred for K8s / CI / GitHub Actions):
#   Paste the raw PEM content of your private key. The runtime writes it to a
#   temp file (0400) at startup. No volume mount needed.
# SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
#   ...key content...
# -----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
#
# Option B (Docker Compose / local with ~/.ssh volume mount):
#   Point to the key file path inside the container.
# SSH_KEY_DIR=~/.ssh   # mounts as /agent/ssh — used with SSH_KEY_PATH=/agent/ssh/id_rsa

# SSH URL of the workflow repo — cloned into /agent/workflow/ on each run.
# Required so the agent can resolve technical_skills/ at runtime.
WORKFLOW_URL=git@github.com:your-org/your-workflow.git

# Go version override — use if 1.25.0 is not yet available on go.dev/dl.
# GO_VERSION=1.24.2

# ── Optional — RAG stack (docker compose --profile rag up) ───────────────────
#
# The RAG stack adds three services: qdrant (vector DB), rag-server (MCP server),
# and indexer (document poller). Agents degrade gracefully when MCP_RAG_URL is
# unset — they run with cold start context and no rag_query tool available.
#
# To enable RAG:
#   1. Uncomment and fill in RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH.
#   2. Set WORKSPACE_ID to your workspace slug (from workspace.yaml).
#   3. Set REPO_PATHS to a comma-separated list of repo paths TO INDEX
#      (paths are inside the container — see volume mounts in docker-compose.yml).
#   4. Run: docker compose --profile rag up --build

# Path to your local clone of the rag-service repo.
# Required by docker compose --profile rag to build rag-server and indexer images.
# RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH=/path/to/your/rag-service

# Workspace ID — partition key for Qdrant tenant isolation.
# Must match workspace_id in workspace.yaml. Example: workspace
# WORKSPACE_ID=workspace

# Comma-separated list of repo paths to index (container-internal paths).
# The indexer mounts workspaces at /repos/workspaces and workflow at /repos/workflow.
# Example: /repos/workspaces/my-workspace,/repos/workflow
# REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspaces/my-workspace,/repos/workflow

# Polling interval in seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes).
# INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=300

# Qdrant URL — defaults to the companion qdrant service in this compose file.
# Override for an external Qdrant instance (e.g. production VM).
# QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333

# RAG server URL passed to agents for MCP context injection.
# Defaults to the companion rag-server in this compose file.
# Set to empty string to disable RAG injection for all agents in this stack.
# MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000

WORKFLOW_URL=git@github.com:your-org/your-workflow.git

# Go version override — use if 1.25.0 is not yet available on go.dev/dl.
# GO_VERSION=1.24.2

# ── Optional — RAG stack (docker compose --profile rag up) ───────────────────
#
# The RAG stack adds three services: qdrant (vector DB), rag-server (MCP server),
# and indexer (document poller). Agents degrade gracefully when MCP_RAG_URL is
# unset — they run with cold start context and no rag_query tool available.
#
# To enable RAG:
#   1. Uncomment and fill in RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH.
#   2. Set WORKSPACE_ID to your workspace slug (from workspace.yaml).
#   3. Set REPO_PATHS to a comma-separated list of repo paths TO INDEX
#      (paths are inside the container — see volume mounts in docker-compose.yml).
#   4. Run: docker compose --profile rag up --build

# Path to your local clone of the rag-service repo.
# Required by docker compose --profile rag to build rag-server and indexer images.
# RAG_SERVICE_LOCAL_PATH=/path/to/your/rag-service

# Workspace ID — partition key for Qdrant tenant isolation.
# Must match workspace_id in workspace.yaml. Example: workspace
# WORKSPACE_ID=workspace

# Comma-separated list of repo paths to index (container-internal paths).
# The indexer mounts workspaces at /repos/workspaces and workflow at /repos/workflow.
# Example: /repos/workspaces/my-workspace,/repos/workflow
# REPO_PATHS=/repos/workspaces/my-workspace,/repos/workflow

# Polling interval in seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes).
# INDEXER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=300

# Qdrant URL — defaults to the companion qdrant service in this compose file.
# Override for an external Qdrant instance (e.g. production VM).
# QDRANT_URL=http://qdrant:6333

# RAG server URL passed to agents for MCP context injection.
# Defaults to the companion rag-server in this compose file.
# Set to empty string to disable RAG injection for all agents in this stack.
# MCP_RAG_URL=http://rag-server:8000
tool_use_result.originalFile:

# GitHub personal access token with `repo` scope.

# Create at: https://github.com/settings/tokens
GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...

# Git identity written into task log entries and claim commits.
# Both agents use the same email (the claim protocol uses SHA not identity).
GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=you@example.com
GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Agent Bot

# ── Optional ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# SSH key delivery — choose one method:
#
# Option A (preferred for K8s / CI / GitHub Actions):
#   Paste the raw PEM content of your private key. The runtime writes it to a
#   temp file (0400) at startup. No volume mount needed.
# SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
#   ...key content...
# -----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
#
# Option B (Docker Compose / local with ~/.ssh volume mount):
#   Point to the key file path inside the container.
# SSH_KEY_DIR=~/.ssh   # mounts as /agent/ssh — used with SSH_KEY_PATH=/agent/ssh/id_rsa

# SSH URL of the workflow repo — cloned into /agent/workflow/ on each run.
# Required so the agent can resolve technical_skills/ at runtime.

# agent.yaml — agent configuration for local testing.
# Copy to agent.yaml and edit the watches list.
# cp agent.yaml.example agent.yaml

# SSH URLs of the workspace management repos this agent watches.
# Add one entry per workspace you want the agent to pick tasks from.
watches:
  - git@github.com:your-org/your-workspace.git

# Set to false to disable the agent without removing the container.
enabled: true

# Maximum random pre-claim jitter in seconds (50 ms – min(500 ms, value * 1000)).
# Higher values reduce contention when many agents start at the same cron tick.
jitter_max_seconds: 2

# Hard budget limits per task activation.
budget:
  # Maximum tokens (input + output) per task. Prevents runaway spend.
  max_tokens_per_task: 100000
  # Maximum tool-use loop iterations per task.
  # 30 is too low for non-trivial implementation tasks — raise as needed.
  max_iterations: 50

# Per-run JSONL event log written to docs/features/<id>/logs/ on the task branch.
log_sink:
  enabled: true

# Seconds to sleep between poll cycles when no eligible task is found,
# and after a successful task run. Controls how frequently the agent checks
# for new work.
#
# Set to 0 for single-shot mode: the process exits after one activation cycle,
# suitable for use with an external scheduler (K8s CronJob, systemd timer, etc.).
#
# Default when omitted: 60
idle_sleep_seconds: 60

#
